edges.note: Both derive from the logical-heading-order / minimum-sizing accessibility baseline.
Flush stacking requires declared edge themes so seams transition cleanly.
Wave-edge transitions are constrained by the one-Wave-graphic-per-email cap.
QA verifies locked ISI body size which lives in the locked End Matter.
Restricts the purple title color to the first line only, no other text.
Both forbid blue/purple in body copy; highlight rule specifies the allowed emphasis treatment.
Sentence-level unity operates on top of the per-role color assignments.
Both govern campaign callout scene-color treatment.
Both define the Flip The Script campaign palette system.
In email the gradient-on-headline allowance is restricted to the two-color split technique; no true gradient text.
Specifies limited application of the primary gradient to headlines/callouts.
Constrains how the defined gradients may be applied in email surfaces.
The single chosen theme must be one of the approved tiles.
Contrast pairings follow from whether the chosen theme tile is light or dark.
Constrains the green accent panel step of the background sequence to a single, exclusive use per email.
The edge-to-edge transition rule governs how the sequenced backgrounds join.
Button flip and heading/body colors are both driven by the same light/dark background group.
Adds the LPGA gating/reservation constraint on top of the general image-to-background locking.
When a populated section is directly above, the image-free path supersedes the right-side image requirement.
Baseline build technique implements the dual desktop/mobile width requirement.
All padding/gutter values are constrained to multiples of the 8-pt base unit.
Content-area width is derived from the 24 px side padding.
Sections rely solely on their own padding since no inter-section margin is allowed.
Both define reserved callout style devices tied to specific verbatim messages.
Related callout banner treatments; scene-paired banners are campaign-specific.
Completeness/no-reorder constraint reinforces the styled trio binding.
Placement/composition applies to the same styled trio section.
CTA section image placement refines the global left-alignment rule by defining where the supporting visual sits relative to the left-aligned text block.
Boxed-warning Heavy weight is the reserved exception within the parent hierarchy.
In email/PPT, Arial substitutes for Avenir in the hierarchy's font mapping.
Enforces the fixed section-type padding by forbidding overrides and spacer rows.
Boundary gaps are the deterministic result of the fixed section padding tokens.
Locked footer build type scale is not restyled by editorial content defaults.
Both govern the locked End Matter/ISI build.
Flip The Script campaign headlines are ALL CAPS instead of default Title Case.
Distinguishes body-copy usage of the campaign name (Title Case) from its all-caps display headline.
Display lockup is the visual treatment; body-copy usage follows the separate Title Case rule.
Prohibited-uses list is the negative complement of the reserved-uses allowlist.
Reinforces bold's reserved role by redirecting prominence needs to subheads/callouts.
The locked-brackets in the mandatory order are the very Top/End Matter that must not be modified.
Clean-edge obligation depends on the fixed positions of the locked Top/End Matter.
Template-formation choice operates within the mandatory assembly sequence.
Content body settings inherit from the locked End Matter ISI body settings.
Reference numbering resolves in/above the locked End Matter.
Content audience is fixed by the supplied locked header.
Both govern DTP patient-facing copy voice and terminology.
Empathetic lead sequencing is a specific application of the everyday-life voice.
Topic-matching and HCP-label exclusion refine the general DTP CTA label conventions.
Poll response buttons still follow the DTP sentence-case label conventions (e.g. "Yes, I have").
